examples.title: Reverse Linked List
examples.description: Iterative algorithm to reverse a singly linked list in-place
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ReverseList(head)
BEGIN
    prev ← NULL
    curr ← head
    WHILE curr ≠ NULL DO
        next ← curr.next
        curr.next ← prev
        prev ← curr
        curr ← next
    END WHILE
    RETURN prev
END
examples.explanation: Three pointers traverse the list. Each node's next pointer is redirected to previous node. At end, prev becomes new head.

examples.title: Find Middle Element
examples.description: Find middle node using slow and fast pointer technique
examples.code:
def find_middle(head):
    slow = head
    fast = head
    
    while fast and fast.next:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next
    
    return slow

# For even length, returns second middle
# For odd length, returns single middle
examples.explanation: Slow pointer moves one node, fast moves two. When fast reaches end, slow is at middle. For even length, returns second of two middle nodes.

examples.title: Detect Cycle - Floyd's Algorithm
examples.description: Detect if linked list has a cycle using two pointers moving at different speeds
examples.explanation: If there's a cycle, fast pointer will eventually lap slow pointer and they will meet. Meeting proves cycle exists.

examples.title: Nth Node From End
examples.description: Find nth node from end using two pointers without finding length
examples.explanation: First pointer goes n steps ahead, then both pointers advance together. When first reaches end, second is at nth node from end.

examples.title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
examples.description: Merge two sorted linked lists into one sorted list
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MergeSorted(list1, list2)
BEGIN
    dummy ← Node(-1)
    tail ← dummy
    
    WHILE list1 ≠ NULL AND list2 ≠ NULL DO
        IF list1.data ≤ list2.data THEN
            tail.next ← list1
            list1 ← list1.next
        ELSE
            tail.next ← list2
            list2 ← list2.next
        END IF
        tail ← tail.next
    END WHILE
    
    IF list1 ≠ NULL THEN
        tail.next ← list1
    ELSE
        tail.next ← list2
    END IF
    
    RETURN dummy.next
END
examples.explanation: Dummy node simplifies head handling. Compare elements from both lists, attach smaller to result. Attach remaining list when other exhausts.

examples.title: Check Palindrome
examples.description: Check if linked list represents a palindrome number
examples.code:
def is_palindrome(head):
    # Step 1: Find middle
    slow = fast = head
    while fast and fast.next:
        slow = slow.next
        fast = fast.next.next
    
    # Step 2: Reverse second half
    prev = None
    curr = slow
    while curr:
        next_node = curr.next
        curr.next = prev
        prev = curr
        curr = next_node
    
    # Step 3: Compare halves
    left = head
    right = prev
    while right:  # Only need to check second half
        if left.val != right.val:
            return False
        left = left.next
        right = right.next
    
    return True
examples.explanation: Find middle, reverse second half in-place, compare first half with reversed second half. For odd length, middle element is automatically skipped.
